mission.title: Install and Configure Antrea on Kubernetes
mission.description: Antrea is a Kubernetes networking solution that provides networking and security services for Kubernetes clusters using Open vSwitch. Installing Antrea allows you to implement efficient Pod networking and enforce Kubernetes Network Policies.

steps.title: Add the Antrea Helm repository
steps.description:
First, add the Antrea Helm chart repository to your Helm configuration:
```bash
helm repo add antrea https://antrea-io.github.io/charts
helm repo update
```

steps.title: Install Antrea using Helm
steps.description:
Now, install Antrea with the following command, specifying the namespace and version:
```bash
helm install antrea antrea/antrea --namespace kube-system --create-namespace --version v2.5.1
```

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
Check if the Antrea pods are running successfully:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n kube-system -l app=antrea
```

steps.title: Configure resource limits for Antrea
steps.description:
To ensure optimal resource usage, you can set resource limits for the Antrea components. Edit the Antrea deployment:
```bash
kubectl edit deployment antrea-controller -n kube-system
``` 
Add the following under the `spec.template.spec.containers.resources` section:
```yaml
resources:
  limits:
    cpu: 500m
    memory: 512Mi
  requests:
    cpu: 200m
    memory: 256Mi
```

resolution.summary: A successful installation of Antrea will show the Antrea pods running in the kube-system namespace. You can verify this by running the `kubectl get pods` command. Additionally, the resource limits should be configured as specified.
codeSnippets:
helm repo add antrea https://antrea-io.github.io/charts
helm repo update
helm install antrea antrea/antrea --namespace kube-system --create-namespace --version v2.5.1

resources:
  limits:
    cpu: 500m
    memory: 512Mi
  requests:
    cpu: 200m
    memory: 256Mi

uninstall.title: Remove the Helm release
uninstall.description:
To cleanly remove the Antrea deployment, use the following command to uninstall the Helm release from the kube-system namespace:
```bash
helm uninstall antrea --namespace kube-system
```

uninstall.title: Clean up CRDs and persistent resources
uninstall.description:
After uninstalling the release, you should remove any Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs) and persistent resources that were created. Run the following commands:
```bash
kubectl delete crd antreanetworking.io
kubectl delete crd antreaegressrules.antrea.io
kubectl delete crd antreanetworkpolicies.antrea.io
```

uninstall.title: Remove namespace and verify
uninstall.description:
If you created a specific namespace for Antrea, you can remove it with the following command. Verify that the namespace has been deleted:
```bash
kubectl delete namespace kube-system
kubectl get namespaces
```

upgrade.title: Backup current state
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, it's good practice to back up the current state of your Antrea installation. You can do this by exporting the current configuration:
```bash
kubectl get deployment antrea-controller -n kube-system -o yaml > antrea-backup.yaml
```

upgrade.title: Update repo and run upgrade command
upgrade.description:
Update the Helm repository and run the upgrade command to upgrade Antrea to the latest version:
```bash
helm repo update
helm upgrade antrea antrea/antrea --namespace kube-system --version v2.6.0
```

upgrade.title: Verify the upgrade
upgrade.description:
After the upgrade, verify that the Antrea pods are running the new version:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n kube-system -l app=antrea
```

troubleshooting.title: Pods stuck in CrashLoopBackOff
troubleshooting.description:
If you notice that the Antrea pods are in a CrashLoopBackOff state, check the logs for more details:
```bash
kubectl logs <pod-name> -n kube-system
```
Look for any error messages that indicate what might be causing the crash.

troubleshooting.title: Network policies not enforced
troubleshooting.description:
If network policies are not being enforced as expected, ensure that the Antrea controller is running:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n kube-system -l app=antrea-controller
```
If it's not running, check the logs for errors.

troubleshooting.title: High resource usage
troubleshooting.description:
If you observe high CPU or memory usage from Antrea components, check the resource limits set in the deployment:
```bash
kubectl get deployment antrea-controller -n kube-system -o yaml
```
Consider adjusting the resource limits based on your cluster's needs.

troubleshooting.title: Antrea not connecting to Open vSwitch
troubleshooting.description:
If Antrea is not connecting to Open vSwitch, check the status of the OVS service:
```bash
kubectl exec -it <antrea-agent-pod-name> -n kube-system -- ovs-vsctl show
```
Ensure that the OVS service is running and properly configured.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running version 1.23 or later, with the NodeIPAMController enabled and Open vSwitch kernel module present on all nodes.
